suites.name: Identity Conformance Suite
suites.description: Runtime and source controls for OIDC authorization, token exchange, introspection, revocation, and Connect scope-enforcement contracts.

familyLabels.I: OIDC authorization, token, userinfo, introspection, revocation, and JWKS contracts.
familyLabels.C: Connect endpoint auth gates, scope gates, CORS, payload limits, and proxy binding.
familyLabels.O: Operational integrity and cross-surface runtime coverage.

familySummary.label: OIDC authorization, token, userinfo, introspection, revocation, and JWKS contracts.
Connect endpoint auth gates, scope gates, CORS, payload limits, and proxy binding.
Operational integrity and cross-surface runtime coverage.

checks.description: Connect notes claim endpoint denies unauthenticated calls.
Connect notes claim endpoint publishes CORS preflight policy.
Connect notes download endpoint denies unauthenticated calls.
Connect notes mint endpoint denies unauthenticated calls.
Connect payments checkout endpoint denies unauthenticated calls.
Connect payments checkout endpoint publishes CORS preflight policy.
Connect payments session endpoint denies unauthenticated calls.
Connect payments session endpoint publishes CORS preflight policy.
Connect record endpoint denies unauthenticated calls.
Connect record endpoint publishes CORS preflight policy.
Connect seal endpoint denies unauthenticated calls.
Connect seal endpoint publishes CORS preflight policy.
Connect transfer endpoint denies unauthenticated calls.
Connect verify endpoint denies unauthenticated calls.
Connect wallet summary endpoint denies unauthenticated calls.
OIDC authorize endpoint rejects missing required parameters.
OIDC introspection endpoint requires authenticated client.
OIDC JWKS endpoint is available with JSON key-set response.
OIDC revocation endpoint requires authenticated client.
OIDC token endpoint rejects unauthenticated authorization_code exchange.
OIDC token endpoint rejects unauthenticated refresh_token exchange.
OIDC userinfo endpoint returns invalid_token without bearer token.
Connect endpoints expose CORS preflight and CORS-wrapped responses.
Connect notes endpoints require mint/claim/read scopes.
Connect mutating endpoints enforce request payload size limits.
Connect payments checkout endpoint requires receiz:payments.create scope.
Connect payments session endpoint requires receiz:payments.read scope.
Connect proxy-style endpoints bind actor user and forward upstream headers explicitly.
Connect record endpoint requires receiz:record scope.
Connect seal endpoint requires receiz:seal scope.
Connect transfer endpoint requires receiz:wallet.transfer scope.
Connect verify endpoint requires receiz:verify scope.
Connect wallet endpoint requires receiz:wallet.read scope.
All referenced source files for identity conformance are present and hashable.
OIDC authorize endpoint enforces PKCE challenge presence.
OIDC authorize endpoint requires normalized redirect URI and allowlist match.
OIDC authorize endpoint parses and validates authorization requests.
OIDC authorize endpoint requires openid scope and allowed scope subset.
OIDC introspection endpoint authenticates client credentials before token status disclosure.
OIDC JWKS endpoint enforces OIDC enabled state and publishes key set.
OIDC protocol helpers provide no-store response contract.
OIDC revocation endpoint implements RFC7009 non-disclosing response behavior.
OIDC token endpoint requires form-encoded body and known grant types.
OIDC token endpoint validates authorization code and PKCE verifier.
OIDC token refresh flow enforces scope subset and openid presence.
OIDC userinfo endpoint requires bearer token and emits invalid_token challenge.
OIDC userinfo endpoint checks access-token revocation and token-use.

suites.name: Issuance Conformance Suite
suites.description: Runtime and source controls for WireProof issuance, AssetSeal custody issuance, and signer enrollment/signing contract integrity.

familyLabels.W: WireProof issuance, webhook auth, key lifecycle, rate limiting, and mint contracts.
familyLabels.A: AssetSeal minting, custody chain continuity, visibility, and append semantics.
familyLabels.S: Signer v1/v4 sign and enroll trust contracts.
familyLabels.O: Operational controls that span issuance surfaces.

familySummary.label: WireProof issuance, webhook auth, key lifecycle, rate limiting, and mint contracts.
AssetSeal minting, custody chain continuity, visibility, and append semantics.
Signer v1/v4 sign and enroll trust contracts.
Operational controls that span issuance surfaces.

checks.description: AssetSeal custody append denies unsigned session calls.
AssetSeal custody list validates code/pulse/anchor coordinate formats.
AssetSeal mint requires signed-in session outside demo mode.
Core issuance conformance endpoints are reachable with expected contract envelopes.
Signer v1 keyring endpoint is reachable and publishes JSON key material metadata.
Signer v1 sign rejects unauthorized requests unless signer configuration is unavailable.
Signer v4 enrollment enforces request contract and origin policy on malformed payloads.
WireProof health endpoint publishes operational/outage JSON contract.
WireProof key creation denies unauthenticated requests.
WireProof key listing denies unauthenticated requests.
WireProof key revoke rejects missing issuer key identifiers.
WireProof mint requires issuer auth or signed-in Pro session.
WireProof webhook rejects unsigned ingress payloads.
AssetSeal canonical claim ensures genesis custody chain continuity for minted assets.
AssetSeal custody append records normalized event pulse/time and transfer field constraints.
AssetSeal custody append links events through prev-hash and canonical event hashing.
AssetSeal custody append requires strict PBI receipt hash contract for event integrity.
AssetSeal custody append is session and premium access gated.
AssetSeal custody list validates code, pulse, and anchor coordinates before ledger reads.
AssetSeal custody list enforces premium gating for private visibility reads.
AssetSeal mint canonicalizes claim content, computes anchor IDs, and persists issuance anchors.
AssetSeal mint requires session and premium access outside demo mode.
All referenced source files for issuance conformance are present and hashable.
Payload size guards and bounded body reads are enforced across issuance write surfaces.
Rate-limit controls are present across issuance, signer service, and enrollment surfaces.
Signer v1 keyring endpoint publishes public keys with explicit cache profile.
Signer v1 sign endpoint enforces bearer authorization and unavailable-state fallback.
Signer v1 sign endpoint validates key existence and retired-key policy.
Signer v1 sign endpoint verifies payload hashes before signing material is used.
Signer v4 enrollment verifies challenge signatures and requires active trusted root signer state.
Signer v4 enrollment validates same-origin policy and request payload contract.
WireProof auth prioritizes issuer key bearer auth then legacy webhook signature fallback.
WireProof issuer secret lookup is hash-based, revocation aware, and updates usage telemetry.
WireProof claim canonicalization emits deterministic kind/version/payment payload structure.
WireProof health probes critical issuance storage tables.
WireProof issuer key creation enforces payload bounds with oversized body rejection.
WireProof issuer key creation is session and Pro-plan gated.
WireProof key listing is session and Pro-plan gated with no-store response headers.
WireProof key revoke endpoint validates request body contract and ownership checks.
WireProof mint computes canonical anchor IDs from code/pulse/claim hash and persists anchors.
WireProof mint JSON response contracts disable caching for runtime issuance artifacts.
WireProof mint enforces issuer/session-aware rate limits and Retry-After signaling.
WireProof mint applies deterministic visibility defaults across demo, issuer-key, and session issuance.
WireProof webhook forwards authenticated payloads to mint JSON path with no-store contract.
WireProof webhook requires signature verification before processing requests.

suites.name: Verification Conformance Suite
suites.description: Runtime and source controls for verification contracts, trusted signature policy, artifact validation, and deterministic verdict behavior.

familyLabels.V: Verify route payload validation, deterministic code generation, and legacy compatibility.
familyLabels.D: Document verify upload gates, file limits, and trusted root-key resolution.
familyLabels.T: Trusted signature v4 policy, key pinning, payload-hash, and certificate-window checks.
familyLabels.G: Artifact trust gates for real Groth16 proofs and anchor coherence.
familyLabels.O: Operational controls shared across verify surfaces.

familySummary.label: Verify route payload validation, deterministic code generation, and legacy compatibility.
Document verify upload gates, file limits, and trusted root-key resolution.
Trusted signature v4 policy, key pinning, payload-hash, and certificate-window checks.
Artifact trust gates for real Groth16 proofs and anchor coherence.
Operational controls shared across verify surfaces.

checks.description: Verify route enforces hard request body limits and emits payload_too_large on overflow.
Verify route validates ts/code input shape and emits deterministic field errors.
Verify route fails closed when signing key is unavailable.
Verify route computes v2 pulse-aware signatures and returns canonical /v slug/code/pulse path.
Verify route preserves legacy v1 compatibility when pulse is absent.
Verify route publishes stable invalid_* and missing_fields error envelopes.
Document verify route enforces IP rate limiting and exposes retry metadata.
Document verify route accepts only multipart file uploads and rejects missing files.
Document verify route enforces request/file size boundaries with 413 file_too_large responses.
Document verify trusted key source prefers server-configured roots and falls back to pinned roots.
Document verify responses are emitted with no-store caching semantics.
Artifact verifier enforces trusted signature requirement when trusted mode is requested.
Artifact verifier reports trusted-mode failure when signature v4 root keys are unavailable.
Artifact verifier requires real g16 proofs with digest/public signal coherence checks.
Artifact verifier enforces anchor bundle/code/pulse coherence in trusted mode.
Signature v4 verifier enforces exact payloadHashSha256 match before signature verification.
Signature v4 verifier enforces root key activation and retirement pulse policy.
Signature v4 verifier enforces certificate chain and signedAt validity windows.
Signature v4 verifier enforces cryptographic verification for the signed payload.
Pinned signature v4 root key list is versioned and available for client verification.
Server signature v4 root key configuration supports env overrides and pinned defaults.
Document limit constants and parsing helpers are centralized and deterministic.
Verify UI surfaces trusted signature state with explicit missing/unavailable/invalid handling.
All referenced source files for verification conformance are present and hashable.
Signer key ID derivation is deterministic and fixed-width hex.
v2 code derivation is deterministic and canonicalized to 8 uppercase alphanumerics.
v2 verification code changes when pulse changes.
Verify route accepts a deterministic valid v2 fixture payload.
Verify route preserves valid legacy v1 fixtures.
Verify route rejects payloads that exceed configured body limits.
Verify route rejects empty payloads with missing_fields.
Document verify route rejects non-multipart requests.
Document verify route rejects multipart payloads without an uploaded file.
Trusted verification root key set resolves from server config or pinned fallback.
Pinned signature v4 root key map is non-empty.
Document verification limits resolve to positive deterministic values.

suites.name: Settlement Conformance Suite
suites.description: Runtime and source controls for deterministic settlement behavior, replay fields, conversion rules, and ledger continuity contracts.

familyLabels.R: Wallet summary and settlement record semantics.
familyLabels.S: Settlement integrity, note minting, and claim gating.
familyLabels.T: Transfer posting, amount validation, nonce, and replay continuity.
familyLabels.L: Ledger continuity across private and public ledger surfaces.

familySummary.label: Wallet summary and settlement record semantics.
Pulse-derived valuation and reference-rate determinism.
Settlement integrity, note minting, and claim gating.
Transfer posting, amount validation, nonce, and replay continuity.
Ledger continuity across private and public ledger surfaces.

checks.description: Wallet interface keeps USD as the user-facing display with supporting phi context.
Wallet summary contract includes custody (`balancePhiMicro`) and USD presentation (`balanceUsd`) fields.
Rate derivation path is pulse-resolved and policy-driven with deterministic quantization.
Conversion uses fixed denominator integer arithmetic with explicit half-up rounding in both directions.
Private ledger route exposes USD, phi, and posting-rate tuple fields.
Public ledger route preserves the same value tuple fields for attestation continuity.
Ledger proof bundle contract includes capsule hash, bundle hash, and verification version identifiers.
Note mint route persists value tuple and idempotency nonce fields.
Note claim route enforces verification gate before claim posting.
Transfer route enforces amount validation and posts value tuple with idempotency nonce.
Public ledger route uses stable pseudonymous actor mapping for identity partition boundaries.
Private ledger route clamps query limits to bounded deterministic windows.
Public ledger route clamps query limits to bounded deterministic windows.
Delegated wallet summary endpoint requires explicit read scope.
Delegated transfer endpoint requires explicit transfer scope.
Canonical pulse resolution is recomputable and non-drifting for identical reference instants.
Rate derivation at a fixed pulse is deterministic and quantizes to a positive microcent integer.
Wallet posting reference rate resolves to a strictly positive integer value.
Phi-to-USD conversion rounds a 0.5 cent boundary up (half-up contract).
USD-to-phi conversion rounds a 0.5 boundary up (half-up contract).
USD -> phi -> USD replay remains within 1 cent tolerance under integer rounding.
All referenced source files for this conformance report are present and hashable.

Payments embed note-claim publishes CORS preflight policy.

Embed checkout restricts callback URLs to HTTPS or localhost HTTP.
Embed checkout allows only USD currency contract.
Embed checkout enforces payload length and JSON decode guards.
Calendar and payment embed builders target their matching API surfaces.

suites.name: World Conformance Suite
suites.description: Runtime and source controls for Receiz World and Public Twin contracts across public routes, disclosure/provenance, promotion gates, and canonical pipeline guarantees.

familyLabels.W: World public-surface, public twin runtime, provenance, policy, promotion, and publisher-session guardrails.

familySummary.label: World public-surface, public twin runtime, provenance, policy, promotion, and publisher-session guardrails.

checks.description: Live publisher-session route denies unauthorized worker calls.
Public profile POST rejects malformed JSON bodies with a stable invalid-payload envelope.
Public profile POST requires a visitor key before welcome generation is allowed.
Public world snapshot route returns no-store JSON when the snapshot is resolved.
Public twin stream POST rejects malformed JSON before opening the SSE channel.
Public twin stream opens an SSE channel with reply-start and terminal event semantics for valid streaming requests.
All referenced source files for world conformance are present and hashable.
Live publisher-session route requires worker authorization before issuing public twin SFU publisher sessions.
World runtime permissions and presentation labels are derived from explicit owner policy and capabilities.
Public profile route multiplexes welcome, message, and booking actions through explicit handlers.
Public profile route enforces IP and visitor rate limits with stable error envelopes.
Public projection helpers expose only the public world and public twin view models.
Public surface inventory publishes world snapshot, profile, stream, and public message routes.
Public world snapshot route uses no-store JSON and surfaces the schema-missing guard.
Approved public twin acceptance gate blocks non-canonical or non-worker photoreal pipelines.
Runtime preserves source and approval lineage while keeping public twin prompts inside guarded action lanes.
World source identity resolution distinguishes canonical avatar-source continuity from fallback surfaces.
Public twin stream route enforces IP and visitor rate limits before opening the stream.
Public twin stream route emits reply lifecycle events over no-store SSE transport.
Synthetic public twin replies are disclosed and signed with provider, source-identity, and approval lineage provenance.
Twin promotion records are persisted as versioned current, avatar, and history manifests.
Pending photoreal twin promotion downgrades public presentation away from live-video promotion.
